text:
Once upon a time, there was an ancient fox named Orion.

Orion loved burrowing holes in trees during rainyweeks. One day, while he digs nostalgically, he finds something shiny. It's an old-looking brass ring. He wears it. People notice.

Years pass—it's Christmas Eve. Orion notices people coming. They stop. Frosty figures. They hang ornaments. It's beautiful. Orion watches quietly. He thinks maybe the best party is just about wearing fancy things.

He takes off his ring—it sparkles! Trees sparkle!

Just an hourglass moon later. Orion takes the ring.

They think fox!

They chase him!

One minute they're angry. Then stars! Orion leaps!

They pause!

Then laugh!

A thousand stars!

He hides.

They find nothing.

They go quiet.

Stars wink!


Oh no!

A snowflake.

They hug.


Every year.

That's our Orion.

Every season.

We believe our friends.

I believe my shiny ring.

So we
